title: 1D DP — ROI & Pattern
oneSentence: Trade memory for time by saving solutions to overlapping subproblems.
description: Master the 1D DP template using Climbing Stairs. Learn top-down memoization and bottom-up tabulation.

roi.complexity: Time: O(N) | Space: O(N) or O(1)

traceSteps.title: Base Case
traceSteps.text: If there is only 1 step, there's only 1 way to climb.

traceSteps.title: Init Pointers
traceSteps.text: Initialize variables for n=1 and n=2 ways.

traceSteps.title: Iterate
traceSteps.text: Compute ways for step 3 up to step n iteratively.

traceSteps.title: Transition
traceSteps.text: Ways to get to step i is the sum of ways to get to (i-1) and (i-2).

traceSteps.title: Shift State
traceSteps.text: Shift the window forward to prepare for the next iteration.

sandbox.question: Why is the space complexity O(1) in this implementation?
options: Because we only store the previous two states instead of an array of size N.
Because DP always takes O(1) space.

followUps.q: What if you can take 1, 2, or 3 steps?
followUps.a: The transition becomes `dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2] + dp[i-3]`. You would need to keep track of the last three states instead of two.
Similar structure, but instead of adding ways, the state transition minimizes cost: `dp[i] = cost[i] + Math.min(dp[i-1], dp[i-2]`.
